name: Vietnamese Crepes
recipeIngredient: 12 oz Orients (heavy cream can be substituted)
2 yellow onions, cut into quarters and then thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the crepe batter the night before serving.
2. Thinly slice green onions.
3. Place one cup of water in a small pan on high heat.
4. Thoroughly blend the 12 oz Bot Banh Xeo mix with the two remaining cups of water until completely moistened.
5. Dissolve sugar in boiling cup of water and add to batter.
6. Add coconut milk and green onions, then mix thoroughly.
7. Refrigerate overnight.
8. The next day, peel and devein shrimp. Cut into halves lengthwise.
9. Cut pork into 2-inch slivers.
10. Cut yellow onion into quarters and then thin slices.
11. Heat a few drops of oil on medium-high heat in a 10-inch heavy frying pan.
12. Add 3 slices of yellow onion and two slices of pork. Cook for a few seconds until the onion is slightly translucent and pork is white.
13. Add 5 shrimp halves and cook for 10 seconds.
14. Pour in half cup of batter, quickly tilting the pan to form a 10-inch circle.
15. Reduce heat to medium. Cover one half crepe with one cup bean sprouts. Cook until the batter looks solid. Cover partially (if you cover completely, water will condense on the crepe) and cook for one minute.
16. Uncover and flip crepe using a spatula. Cook until slightly crisp (approximately 1 minute).
17. Serve immediately to keep warm. Serve with fresh cilantro, mint, or basil leaves (optional) and large leaves of red lettuce.
18. To eat, tear off a piece of crepe, fill with herbs, and wrap in lettuce leaf. Dip in sauce and eat.
Serves 4.
